mnemonics: P.A.S.S. Objects: Pass And Share State. When you pass an object as a parameter, you are sharing its state (data) with the method.
keyPoints: Objects are passed by reference, meaning the method receives a copy of the reference to the object, not a copy of the object itself.
Changes made to the object's state inside the method will be reflected in the original object outside the method.
This allows methods to directly manipulate and modify the state of the objects passed to them.
commonMistakes: Assuming objects are passed by value (like primitives), leading to unexpected modifications of the original object.
Forgetting that 'pass by reference' applies to the object's state, not the reference variable itself—reassigning the parameter inside the method does not change the original reference.
